name: wolfgang-indian-mact-drafting
display_name: wolfgang_rush — Motor Accident Claims Tribunal Drafting · Claude Desktop Extension (India)
description: Drafting connector for Indian Motor Accident Claims Tribunals. STRICT SIX-AGENT PIPELINE: Reader → Format → Drafter → Verifier → Refiner → Overseer, producing six named artifacts (case-facts.md, format-shell.md, draft-v1.docx, verification-report.md, draft-v2.docx, final-draft.docx). MANDATORY INVOCATION ORDER: (1) call get_agent_instructions() with NO arguments FIRST to receive the full orchestration script, (2) follow every step in that script in order, (3) save every intermediate artifact via save_artifact, (4) save final-draft.docx via save_draft_as_docx. DO NOT write standalone python-docx or JavaScript scripts to generate output — invoke the pipeline tools directly. DO NOT skip the Verifier (anti-hallucination firewall) or the Overseer (opposing-counsel lens). Reader applies the privacy firewall — party names, addresses, identifying numbers, financial figures substituted with structural placeholders before any downstream agent processes the facts. Zero data leaves the user's machine.
long_description: wolfgang_rush — Motor Accident Claims Tribunal Drafting is a local-execution MCPB Desktop Extension for the full MV Act 1988 (as amended by MV Amendment Act 2019) claims architecture. Ten case-type templates spanning Section 166 fault-based petitions, Section 164 lump-sum no-fault, Section 161 solatium and hit-and-run Solatium Fund scheme, Section 147 third-party insurance, Section 173 High Court appeals, Section 174 collector-recovery, MV Act criminal defence under BNSS, MACT interim compensation and claim-execution applications, plus Workmen's Compensation under the Employee's Compensation Act 1923. Sarla Verma + Pranay Sethi multiplier doctrine encoded.

tools.name: list_case_types
tools.description: List all MACT case types this connector can draft (10 case types spanning MV Act and Workmen's Compensation).

tools.name: get_case_type_format
tools.description: Get the drafting format for a specific MACT case type.

tools.name: get_agent_instructions
tools.description: MANDATORY FIRST CALL with no arguments — returns the full orchestration script with every step and tool call enumerated. Optional agent_name argument returns that single agent's persona instructions.

tools.name: get_pleading_base
tools.description: Get the shared MACT drafting base structure.

tools.name: list_states
tools.description: List the State exemplars available for MACT drafting (priority 8 States).

tools.name: get_state_exemplar
tools.description: Get the State-specific exemplar for MACT (solatium-fund variations, State Government notifications, State High Court procedural notes).

tools.name: read_case_folder
tools.description: Read all files in a case folder on the user's machine.

tools.name: save_draft_as_docx
tools.description: Save a markdown draft as a filing-grade .docx using pandoc.

tools.name: create_case_folder
tools.description: Create the case folder for this drafting session on the user's machine under ~/Downloads/Wolfgang-Rush-Drafts/. Returns the folder path with an inputs/ subfolder.

tools.name: save_artifact
tools.description: Save a pipeline artifact (case-facts.md, format-shell.md, draft-v1.md, verification-report.md, draft-v2.md, opposing-notes.md, or any input document) into the case folder. Required at the end of every agent step.
